encounter.location: UNC Medical Center, 101 Manning Drive, Chapel Hill, NC 27514

labs.name: fractional exhaled nitric oxide (FeNO)
labs.interpretation: mildly elevated but improved, suggesting improved eosinophilic inflammation control

conditions.name: severe persistent asthma
conditions.note: Improved on Advair HFA 115/21 BID plus montelukast. FeNO improved from 38 to 24 ppb. Spirometry shows mild obstruction with borderline bronchodilator response. Rescue inhaler use approximately twice weekly. Discussed omalizumab as potential next step once patient turns 6 in March 2026. Continuing current regimen with reassessment in 3–4 months.

conditions.name: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis
conditions.note: Peanut-specific IgE remains very high at 41.6 kU/L. Oral food challenge not appropriate. Discussed peanut oral immunotherapy (Palforzia protocol) with family. Decision pending.

conditions.name: tree nut allergy (cashew, walnut, almond)
conditions.note: Specific IgE values trending down but still significantly positive. Continue strict avoidance. No change in management.

conditions.name: atopic dermatitis
conditions.note: Near remission. Minimal dry patches on bilateral antecubital fossae. Continue daily moisturizer only.

conditions.name: allergic rhinitis
conditions.note: Well controlled on cetirizine 5 mg daily.

medications.name: fluticasone/salmeterol (Advair HFA) 115/21 mcg inhaler
medications.sig: 1 puff inhaled twice daily via spacer

medications.name: albuterol (ProAir HFA) 90 mcg inhaler
medications.sig: 2 puffs inhaled as needed for wheezing/shortness of breath via spacer

medications.name: epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen Jr) 0.15 mg
medications.sig: as needed for anaphylaxis, carry at all times
medications.reason: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis

medications.name: triamcinolone 0.1% ointment
medications.sig: apply to eczema flares twice daily as needed

document_references.description: Comprehensive allergy and asthma review note documenting interval history, vitals, FeNO, spirometry results, specific IgE panel results, physical exam findings, and plan including discussion of omalizumab and peanut oral immunotherapy.
Omalizumab (Xolair) educational materials provided to mother regarding potential biologic therapy for asthma management once patient becomes age-eligible in March 2026.
Updated anaphylaxis emergency action plan reflecting current allergens (peanut, cashew, walnut, almond), current medications, and emergency instructions for epinephrine administration.

diagnostic_reports.type: spirometry with pre- and post-bronchodilator testing

allergies.note: Confirmed by specific IgE and skin prick testing. No known ingestion reaction.
